hard.meaning: Blocks release because failure creates legal, compliance, security, build, routing, source, or publication-integrity risk.

strong_soft.meaning: Does not block artifact handoff, but must be visible because it materially affects production readiness or operator follow-through.

warning.meaning: Advisory quality or optimization issue. It should not block release unless it accumulates into a strategic or user-facing risk.

pettyBlockerPolicy: Cosmetic preferences, optional metadata improvements, minor wording preferences, and low-impact optimization gaps may warn but must not hard-fail release.

validators.risk: Content automation cannot run without a strategy gate and forbidden-claim boundaries.
Pre-license site cannot publish licensed treatment claims or activate sensitive capabilities.
Approved scheduled content must be complete, dated, source-aware, disclaimer-aware, and eligible before autopublish.
Required legal pages and mode-specific disclaimers must exist.
Critical accessibility affordances must exist for crisis, forms, cookie controls, and animated hero behavior.
Workflows must not be configured to fail before validation or mutate governance files at runtime.
Admin cannot be represented as production-secure without real auth boundary and write-model warnings.
AEO/SEO/GEO strategy must include query, answer, entity, route, atom, market, and public answer surfaces.
Strategic photo slots must include representation, alt text, placement, forbidden categories, and Dianne-photo separation.
Repo Work OS authority files, contracts, content systems, proof packets, and validation boundaries must be audited.
Approved posts must be clickable from the blog index and render real content pages with atoms, FAQs, and disclaimers.
Admin must expose content controls while preserving Dianne photo, licensure switch, and resources admin functions.
Repo authority, contracts, generated surfaces, workflows, and required files must be present.
Missing lockfile means local or CI dependency resolution is not reproducible until install succeeds.
Future self-produced posts after 2026-12-31 must have self-healing policy, margin of error, and nonblocking word-count/style handling.
Style, metadata, and optional extraction improvements should be visible without becoming petty blockers.
